Progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) is caused by the JC virus. Currently, there are no approved vaccines for PML or the JC virus. While there have been sporadic case reports of experimental therapeutic vaccinations (e.g., using JC virus capsid protein VP1) going back over a decade, none have advanced to general availability or FDA approval, and the consensus in recent literature up to November 2025 is that there are no approved therapeutics or vaccines for it. Given that vaccine development and approval takes years, and there are no late-stage vaccine candidates in the active pipeline nearing approval within the next 3 months, it is extremely unlikely that a vaccine will be developed and the Wikipedia article updated to "Yes" by the resolution dates in December 2025, January 2026, or March 2026. The probability remains at the absolute floor of random Wikipedia edits or extremely unheralded announcements. I am assigning the minimum configurable probability of 0.05 for the first two dates and a nominally higher 0.06 for the furthest date.

The only way this resolves 'Yes' is due to unnoticed Wikipedia vandalism on the exact resolution dates, which is highly unlikely for a monitored medical list page. The probabilities are kept near the absolute floor of 0.02, with a slight increase to 0.03 for the furthest date to reflect minimally increasing uncertainty over time as per instructions.

Zhang Lin holds the 800m freestyle long course world record (7:32.12), set during the super-suit era in 2009. The closest active swimmers (e.g., Hafnaoui, Short, Schwarz, and Wiffen) remain roughly 5-6 seconds away from this time, with the fastest recent times being over 7:37. A May 2025 Swimming World article described the record as 'out of reach.' Furthermore, the 2025 World Aquatics Championships already took place in July/August, and the short-term forecast horizon (Dec 2025 to Mar 2026) mainly coincides with the short-course season and early/off-peak long-course season, heavily reducing the opportunity for peak long-course performances. The historical 5-second gap to the world record is too large to realistically be closed in off-peak meets within the next three months. We cap the probability at the allowed 0.95 maximum and slightly decrease it for longer horizons to satisfy the increasing uncertainty constraint.

There is currently no approved vaccine for Chromoblastomycosis, a rare fungal infection, and the scientific consensus (StatPearls 2024, etc.) confirms there are no commercial vaccines available or in late-stage clinical trials. Developing and approving a new vaccine, especially for a fungal disease (where no approved human vaccines currently exist), takes many years. It is highly implausible that a vaccine would be developed, approved, and added to the English Wikipedia's 'List of infectious diseases' within the 1-to-3-month forecast horizon. Therefore, the probability is set to the lowest permissible bound, increasing marginally for the later dates to reflect time horizon uncertainty.

Vaccine development is a years-long process, and there are currently no human vaccines for Hemolytic-uremic syndrome (HUS) or its primary cause, E. coli O157:H7, nearing approval in the next 3 months. The Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' as of 2025-12-07 shows an empty entry for the HUS vaccine, aligning with the "No" status. Given the very short forecast horizons (7 days, 1 month, and 3 months), the probability of a vaccine being successfully developed, approved, and added to the Wikipedia page on these resolution dates is extremely close to zero. Some slight uncertainty is added for the more distant dates to account for potential Wikipedia vandalism or misinterpretation of animal E. coli vaccines by editors, but the true base rate of vaccine development in this short window is virtually nil.

The question asks if a vaccine for Balantidiasis will have been developed by 2025-12-14, 2026-01-06, and 2026-03-07, based on the Wikipedia article "List of infectious diseases". Currently, the Wikipedia article lists "Balantidiasis" with an empty "Vaccine(s)" cell. Balantidiasis is a rare/neglected protozoan infection caused by Balantidium coli. My search for vaccine development returned sources explicitly stating there is no vaccine (e.g., "As of now, there are no vaccines specifically for balantidiasis"). Treatment relies on antibiotics like tetracycline or metronidazole. Developing a new vaccine takes years and would be widely reported in scientific literature before approval or addition to Wikipedia. Since there is no ongoing vaccine in late-stage clinical trials for this rare parasitic disease, the probability that the Wikipedia table will update to include a vaccine by March 2026 is extremely low. I assign it a bare minimum probability of 2% for the near-term dates and 3% for the slightly more distant March 2026 date to account for potential vandalism on Wikipedia or an extremely obscure development we missed, but realistically it's as close to zero as possible.

The Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' currently features an empty cell (which counts as 'No' according to the criteria) for Clostridioides difficile colitis under the Vaccine(s) column. A review published in PubMed in May 2025 explicitly states that there is currently no approved vaccine on the market for C. difficile. Pfizer's Phase 3 CLOVER trial for its vaccine candidate failed its primary endpoint in 2022 and there is no indication that any company has submitted a Biologics License Application (BLA) for a C. difficile vaccine. Given the standard ~10 month FDA review time, it is virtually impossible for a vaccine to be approved and added to Wikipedia by March 2026. The only pathway to a 'Yes' resolution would be persistent Wikipedia vandalism, which is highly unlikely. Therefore, the probabilities are set to the minimum allowable bounds, slightly increasing over time due to general uncertainty.

The stock (ADI) closed at an all-time high of $279.43 on December 5, 2025, following a massive ~25% rally triggered by strong Q4 earnings, analyst upgrades, and AI/semiconductor momentum. However, this creates a high bar to clear. Firstly, the stock goes ex-dividend on Monday, December 8, which will technically and mechanically reduce the stock price by the $0.99 dividend amount, meaning it will begin the forecasting period running with an immediate handicap. Furthermore, such vertical movement usually suggests short-term overbought conditions that often lead to profit-taking, sideways consolidation, or mean reversion in the coming days/weeks. The historical fetch API estimated the probability of remaining elevated at only ~0.39. There is also an impending FOMC meeting (Dec 9-10) combined with inflation data that will introduce significant volatility. Thus, for the first target date (Dec 14), the combination of mean reversion, the ex-dividend drop, and macro uncertainty strongly points to a lower probability of surpassing the $279.43 target (0.32). As the horizon broadens to 1 month (Jan 06) and 3 months (Mar 07), uncertainty naturally increases. Given the overarching positive fundamentals for ADI in 2026 and standard market upward drift, the probabilities mean-revert towards 0.5 over time, yielding 0.41 and 0.46 respectively.

Stock movements over short windows behave largely as a random walk with drift. The physical drift of a stock's price is the expected total return (historic equity premium) minus its dividend yield. For a REIT like FRT, total expected return (~9%) minus the dividend yield (4.6%) gives an expected annualized nominal price drift of roughly +4.4%. Over 1 week (Dec 14 horizon), the dividend has no effect, yielding a slight positive drift that slightly tips the scales above 0.5 (estimated Z-score ~ +0.06 => p~0.51). However, the second horizon (Jan 6) falls immediately after FRT's expected ex-dividend date on January 2, 2026. This discrete dividend payment of $1.13 will drop the nominal price mechanically by over 1.1%, offsetting the modest upward long-term drift and resulting in a negative expected price change for that horizon (p~0.46). For the third horizon (March 7), two additional months of expected positive market returns will have accrued without a second ex-dividend date (the next is likely early April), restoring the total expected nominal return to a small positive value (~ +1%), pulling the probability back above 50% but maintaining high uncertainty (p~0.52).

1. Anchor: Following the instructions, the yfinance tool’s statistical P(increase) estimate of 0.44 serves as the primary probability anchor for the near-term (Dec 14).
2. Additional Context: The web search confirms highly bearish sentiment. CarMax recently suffered a ~24% stock drop on November 6 following the unexpected departure of its CEO and a weak preliminary Q3 outlook. Competitors like Carvana are significantly outperforming, and securities fraud class-action lawsuits were announced on December 3. Furthermore, since KMX has been a massive underperformer in 2025, it is highly susceptible to year-end "tax-loss harvesting" selling pressure throughout December, keeping the odds of a price increase below 50%.
3. Time Horizon Scaling: The Q3 earnings announcement will occur on December 18. By the January 6 and March 7 resolution dates, the initial shock of the CEO's departure and bad earnings may be fully priced in, potentially leading to stabilization or a "January effect" mean-reversion bounce. As weekly stock moves are largely unpredictable random walks over longer periods, uncertainty must increase over time. I therefore regress the initial 0.44 probability back toward 0.50 (0.47 for Jan 6, 0.49 for Mar 7) for the medium-term horizons.

The baseline price for all resolution dates is the market close on 2025-12-05 (the last trading day before the forecast date), which is approximately $292.02. This represents a recent sharp drop from an all-time high of $307.63 on December 3, likely due to guidance or remarks provided at the Barclays Eat, Sleep, Play, Shop Conference on December 4. Institutional price targets (such as Goldman Sachs maintaining a $288 PT) sit slightly below the current price, indicating limited immediate upside. 

For the short-term horizon (Dec 14), negative momentum and negative seasonal trends make further slight decline more likely. The YFinance statistical tool gives a 0.43 combined probability for an increase. I place the Dec 14 estimate at 0.44.
For the medium-term (Jan 6) and long-term (Mar 7) horizons, market dynamics suggest a wider array of possibilities and mean reversion into standard slightly positive stock market drift. However, per the strict instructions to increase uncertainty over time (meaning distant probabilities must step successively closer to 0.5), I temper the probabilities while still acknowledging the subdued institutional outlook (PT of 288). 
Thus, the probabilities are set strictly to reduce their absolute distance from 0.5 as the horizon increases: 0.44 (|D|=0.06), 0.47 (|D|=0.03), and 0.49 (|D|=0.01).

The primary statistical anchor provided by the time series tool is P(increase) = 0.39, reflecting a broader negative trend since September 2025 despite the recent late-November/early-December bounce. The stock has rallied sharply from ~$588 to $672 on the back of extremely positive news regarding 30% cuts to reality labs (metaverse) spending and more rationalized CapEx. While Wall Street is universally bullish on this cost turnaround—bringing strong immediate momentum—the $672 level represents a local peak after a very fast 15%+ run and could see near-term profit taking, aligning with the statistical model's lower base rate. Balancing the model's 0.39 bearish anchor with the extremely bullish fundamental catalyst and the instruction to stay close to 0.5, I set the short-term probability at 0.45. Forecasting forward, standard market drift and increasing uncertainty suggest probabilities should regress toward 0.5, pushing the 1-month and 3-month estimates to 0.47 and 0.49 respectively.

The yfinance tool generated a combined statistical estimate of 0.56 for Nike's stock to exceed the Dec 7 threshold by Dec 14. This is supported by recent upward momentum over the last two weeks, where the stock climbed from ~$60 to ~$65.43, partly in response to recent leadership changes by the CEO in early December. For the first resolution date (Dec 14), which falls right before the Q2 earnings release on Dec 18, I maintain a slight bullish bias derived from the statistical anchor, setting it at 0.54. Because earnings reactions are highly unpredictable and the turnaround is still considered a work in progress, variance will increase significantly after Dec 18. Following the instruction to compress further-out dates toward 0.5 due to increasing uncertainty over forecast horizons, I assign 0.52 to January 6 and 0.51 to March 7.

The baseline value is the close on Friday, Dec 5, 2025, which we identified as $225.76. The stock experienced a massive 15%+ narrative-driven spike from ~$193 in late November up to $225.76 in just a few days following AI-optimistic news and a dividend announcement. The statistical trend analysis gives a predicted P(>225.8) of 0.39 for the short term, largely because this price sits at the upper resistance of its 52-week range and deviates significantly from the 3-month downward trend line. In addition, the ex-dividend date is Dec 10 for a $1.01 dividend, which will mechanically drop the share price by roughly that amount prior to the first resolution date on Dec 14. This strongly reinforces a below-0.5 probability for the shortest horizon. Over longer time horizons (Jan 6 and Mar 7), broader market structural drift works in the stock's favor, meaning the probabilities should progressively regress toward 0.5 as uncertainty increases.

The `fetch_ts_yfinance` tool provided a combined statistical estimate of 0.56 for the probability of the stock exceeding its current 75.31 threshold in the short term. OKE has seen strong upward momentum recently, jumping from ~68 in mid-November to 75.31 on December 5, largely in part due to its $1.2B sale of pipeline systems. I anchor my initial 1-week forecast at 0.56 following this strong recent momentum and statistical prior. For the distant dates, while general market drift is positive, there is significant mean-reversion risk following a large rapid advance, plus OKE has experienced a broader downward trend over the past year (down ~25%). Additionally, OKE typically has an ex-dividend date in early February (approx. $1.03 per share), which mechanically lowers the stock price ahead of the March 2026 resolution date. In accordance with the instruction that uncertainty should increase and probabilities should move closer to 0.5 for more distant targets, I steadily taper my estimates toward 0.50.

The current threshold price (from Friday, Dec 5) is ~$162, following a sharp 5% rally over three days prompted by the company's December 3 announcement of an 8% dividend increase. The Yahoo Finance statistical trend model gives a 1-week probability of roughly 0.39 for the stock to remain above this localized spike, largely due to likely mean reversion and a slightly negative 60-day linear trend. Because short-term price movements off a sharp jump tend to experience downward pressure (profit-taking) in the immediate term, our 1-week forecast is set at 0.42. Following the specific instructions that uncertainty should increase with the forecast horizon and probabilities must drift towards 0.50, the subsequent probabilities are increased toward the coin-flip baseline as the mean-reversion edge fades over the 1-month and 3-month horizons.

The stock has seen a sharp short-term positive run-up in early December 2025, moving from ~$35 up to ~$38.23 by Dec 5. This was catalyzed by news of SLB's AI digital product growth, ChampionX integration, and OPEC+ delaying Q1 production increases. Historically, short-term momentum favors continuation, and our statistical tool gives a combined 56% probability of being higher over a 1-week horizon, though the linear trend model sits lower at 46% due to the high mean-reversion risk following a rapid 8%+ price gap. Broad equity market baseline implies a ~51-53% chance of being higher at any given point due to market drift. As the forecast horizon extends to January and March 2026, stock prices approach a random walk relative to current information, pulling our probabilities progressively closer to the 0.50 state of maximum uncertainty.

The ex-dividend date for SRE's upcoming dividend ($0.645 per share) is confirmed to be Thursday, December 11, 2025. Because the reference date is December 7, 2025 (which will use the closing price from Friday, December 5), the reference price will include the upcoming dividend, while all three resolution dates will not. 

This creates a ~$0.645 (or roughly 0.7%) headwind acting against the stock being higher on the resolution dates. 
For 2025-12-14 (one week after the reference date), the stock has only a few days to overcome this 0.7% deficit. Thus, the probability of exceeding the reference price is significantly below 0.5 (estimated at 0.38).
For 2026-01-06 (one month out), typical utility-stock upward drift may offset some of the dividend drop, but the expected price change net of dividend is near zero, and volatility covers up the gap, pushing the probability closer to 0.5 (0.45).
For 2026-03-07 (three months out), standard positive equity drift over an entire quarter offsets the $0.645 dividend drop. The expected price change becomes slightly positive, and increased volatility dominates, resulting in a probability faintly above but much closer to 0.5 (0.52), successfully obeying the rule that uncertainty must increase (be closer to 0.5) over longer horizons.

Teledyne's stock jumped recently to 522.30 on December 5, 2025, which sets a high bar for future prices. The 60-day linear trend has been downward over the past few months, dropping from a peak of nearly 580 in September. The jump to 522 appears associated with a recent defense contract announcement, but short-term stock movements often mean-revert following rapid spikes. Given this context and the statistical base rate suggesting a downward bias for the immediate future (with P(higher) estimated at 0.39), the 1-week forecast is set below 50%. In accordance with standard forecasting principles for financial markets, uncertainty dominates over longer time horizons, so the probabilities revert toward 0.50 for the 1-month and 3-month prediction dates.

TER closed at a record high of $200.69 on Dec 5, following an extreme one-week surge from $179.55 on Dec 1, driven by AI optimism and an analyst upgrade to a $225 price target. The short-term statistical model predicts a high risk of mean reversion given the steep parabolic move and 'unfilled gaps' left in the chart between $180 and $189. Pullbacks are common after such explosive rallies, making the stock more likely than not to close below $200.69 within the 7-day window (P1 = 0.42). 

As the forecast horizon extends to January 6, the short-term overbought conditions may resolve, and the stock’s upward AI momentum provides stronger support. Thus, the probability drifts back toward a random walk (P2 = 0.47). 

By March 7 (3 months out), TER will have reported Q4 earnings. The high execution risk priced into the record valuation directly offsets the general positive drift of equities. Long-term forecasting reverts to maximal uncertainty, meaning distant outcomes should sit closest to a coin toss with a tiny bias for the equity risk premium (P3 = 0.51).

The base rate expectation for a stock after 1 week, 1 month, and 3 months is roughly 51%, 53%, and 56% respectively based on market drift. However, TKO has just experienced an exceptional run-up in its stock price over the last two weeks, jumping from 175 to an all-time high area around 200.65 on Friday Dec 5. A statistical trend analysis estimates the chance of being above this specific threshold in a week at roughly 39%, reflecting high likelihood of short-term mean reversion or profit-taking. There is no major isolated news catalyst explicitly driving the immediate Dec 4 spike besides a $0.78 dividend announcement, although fundamental momentum is generally strong (Q3 guidance raised, EPS growth, WWE on Netflix starting Jan 2026). The ex-date for the dividend is likely Dec 12 or 15, which will mechanically drop the price by roughly $0.78, providing a very slight downward headwind for the short-to-medium term resolutions. Because of the strong mean reversion effect expected to act on an acute 10%+ spike over just a week, I place the 1-week probability heavily downwards towards 42%. Over 1 month (January 6), the effect of the Netflix deal kickoff could offer support, so I adjust probability to 46%. By March (3 months out), underlying market baseline and equity drift take over, putting the probability near balanced (51%) where a breakout could be sustained or reversion completes.

As forecast horizons increase (to 1 month and 3 months), the impact of short-term momentum fades and movements become guided by a random walk, meaning probabilities should regress toward 0.5 per the system's instructions on long-horizon uncertainty. We assign a 45% probability for the Dec 14 resolution, 48% for the January resolution, and 50% for the March resolution.

The `fetch_ts_yfinance` tool provides a statistical baseline suggesting a 56% probability that the price on December 14 will exceed the December 7 baseline of $53.84. This combined estimate weights seasonal tendencies over a mildly negative linear trend over the last 60 days. Q3 2025 earnings showed strong operational performance (+7.2% net sales, +3.9% comp store sales), and the ex-dividend date for the December 9 payout occurred well before the December 7 baseline, meaning no mechanical payout-related drop will affect this evaluation window. Given the specific instruction that uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon and probabilities for distant dates should pull closer to 0.5, the estimates steadily revert toward a 50% coin toss for January and March 2026.

The fetch_ts_yfinance tool retrieved recent closing prices for TXN, showing a sharp rally from ~152 in mid-November to 181.36 on December 5. The combined statistical estimate predicted a 0.56 probability of exceeding this threshold in the short term, driven by robust recent momentum and seasonal strength. However, the linear trend over the past 3-4 months is negative, and a web search highlighted that overall fundamentals remain weak due to prolonged chip market slumps, tariff uncertainties, and heavy CapEx (a $60 billion "fab bet").

Given these factors, the short-term forecast (Dec 14) favors the recent upward momentum and verified institutional buying (giving a slight edge at 0.54). For the January 6 date, momentum effects typically begin to fade on a 1-month horizon, bringing the estimate closer to the base rate (0.52). Over a 3-month horizon (Mar 7), TXN will undergo its Q4 earnings report, which inherently carries high variance, and face a mechanically negative impact from an expected ~$1.42 late-January ex-dividend date, compounded by potential new US tariff implementations. As temporal uncertainty increases and specific mid-term bearish tailwinds appear, the confidence in predicting whether the price will end higher or lower than the recent peak of 181.36 decreases to 0.50. This aligns with the efficient market hypothesis and the directive that forecasts for more distant horizons should regress toward 0.5.

The current reference stock price is $83.43 (from 2025-12-05 via the yfinance tool, as 2025-12-07 is a Sunday). 
1. Base rate & Initial Anchor: The yfinance statistical tool provides a combined estimate of 0.39 for the stock being higher on the first resolution date, derived from a slightly negative 60-day drift and unfavorable seasonal prior (0/1 past year). Acknowledging that weekly stock moves are mostly random and the tool cautions to stay close to 0.50, I anchored the first date slightly higher at 0.44. 
2. Catalysts & Fundamentals: Information from Q3 2025 earnings is strong (EPS up, backlog growth), and analyst targets average above $90, which indicates potential undervaluation. However, standard consensus is primarily a 'Hold'. 
3. Horizon Effects: By standard unpredictable market dynamics and explicit instructions, uncertainty must increase over greater horizons, demanding that the probability profile drifts cleanly toward 0.5. At 1-month I estimate 0.48, and at 3-months, capturing standard positive market drift but mostly random walk characteristics, I estimate 0.51, reflecting increasing uncertainty.

The default yfinance tool failed, so historical GBM principles and recent news were analyzed. Over short and medium horizons, stocks exhibit a slight positive drift dictated by their expected return (for UDR, CAPM suggests roughly 9% annualized). Although analysts slightly cut targets on Dec 1, 2025 (Truist to $41, RBC to $38), these targets remain substantially above the current ~$36 trading range, implying perceived underlying equity value and positive expected drift. 

For the 1-week horizon (Dec 14), there are no major mechanical downward drags, so the positive drift keeps the probability slightly above 0.5 (set to 0.52). 
For the 1-month horizon (Jan 6), the price avoids the Q4 ex-dividend date (which historical patterns place around Jan 8-9) and accrues dividend value, but the strict instruction to increase uncertainty and move closer to 0.5 brings the estimate down to 0.51.
For the 3-month horizon (Mar 7), the stock will have absorbed the ex-dividend drop of ~$0.43 (approx 1.2% reduction in price). This mechanical drop offsets much of the expected 3-month drift. Corresponding with both the real dampening effect of the dividend payout and the overarching instruction to pull distant probabilities closer to 0.5 as uncertainty rises, the probability is set cleanly to 0.50.

The target threshold is VLO's closing price on Dec 5, 2025 (since Dec 7 is a Sunday), which was 173.08. The stock has experienced strong downward momentum over the past week, falling from ~179 to 173 amid broader market weakness. This short term technical downtrend and the statistical trend analysis (estimating P=0.43 for the next week) suggest a slightly higher chance of the stock remaining below the threshold in the very short term (Dec 14).

For the Jan 6 timeframe, strong fundamental signals from recent analyst upgrades (Piper Sandler and Wells Fargo assigning price targets of $220+) should provide a fundamental tailwind, balancing the short term weakness and bringing the probability closer to normal.

By Mar 7, although general equity market drift tends upward, an established annual ex-dividend event expected in late January (~$1.13 payout) will mechanically subtract from the unadjusted share price, offsetting normal drift. Furthermore, following the instruction to increase uncertainty and bring distant dates closer to 0.5, the final estimate rests at exactly 0.5.

The market close price for 2025-12-07 (a Sunday) corresponds to the previous trading day's close on Friday, 2025-12-05, which was 219.96. Following Workday's Q3 fiscal 2026 earnings on November 25, the stock experienced a material drop from ~$233 to ~$215 due to slight downward guidance on subscription revenue. Over the last week (Dec 1-5), the stock has exhibited mean reversion and positive momentum, climbing from the low 213s back to 219.96. The statistical time series model evaluated a probability of 0.58 for exceeding 220 next week, factoring in this positive short-term momentum. However, considering the overarching instruction that weekly stock moves are largely unpredictable barring extremely strong signals, and that probabilities for more distant horizons should regress closer to 0.5 to reflect increasing uncertainty, the estimates are kept only slightly above 0.50. I assign a 54% probability for the 1-week horizon (capturing the recent upward post-earnings rebound momentum), decaying towards 0.52 and 0.51 for the 1-month and 3-month horizons respectively.

WDC's stock just hit a massive recent high of 168.80 on December 5 after a rapid ascent driven by strong AI storage demand and increased analyst targets (e.g., Citi raising its target to $200). Since December 7 falls on a Sunday, the comparison price is exactly this recent peak of 168.80, which reflects a 5% single-day jump. This makes short-term mean reversion highly probable. The fetch_ts_yfinance combined model returns an estimated 0.43 probability of exceeding this peak for the immediate 1-week horizon (Dec 14). I anchor to this 0.43 for Dec 14. Over slightly longer time horizons (Jan 6 and Mar 7), the effect of a specific daily peak fades, and the baseline equity risk premium combined with the strong underlying AI storage momentum naturally brings the probability closer to the 50% baseline. I trend the probabilities to 0.46 for Jan 6 and to 0.49 for Mar 7, obeying the instruction that probabilities for distant dates should approach 0.5.

The target price anchor is 138.54, which was the close price on Friday, Dec 5 (as the forecast date of Dec 7 falls on a weekend, the previous close is used). Based on YFinance historical data, the statistical trend analysis and seasonal comparison provide a combined probability estimate of 0.44 for the near-term date. This serves as our primary anchor, supported by recent slight negative momentum (-0.02 per day slope and a recent 1.3% one-day dip). For longer horizons, I adjust the probabilities to converge closer to 0.5, representing increased uncertainty over time as noise overtakes the short-term momentum. Fundamentally, Xylem maintains a strong outlook following a Q3 earnings beat and margin inflection narratives, supporting a reversion toward a near-50% base rate for Q1 2026.
